claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 9 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 9 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

Let's start: **How many children are you looking to claim Child Benefit for, and what are their names and ages?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit for my two children. Alex is 8 years old and Blake is 9 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's great to meet you. Let me ask a few more questions to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex and Blake.

**Are both Alex and Blake currently living with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, both Alex and Blake live with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you.

**Are you responsible for the day-to-day care of both Alex and Blake?** (For example, providing their food, clothing, and general welfare.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I take care of both of them - their meals, clothes, school things, everything they need day-to-day.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Wonderful, thank you for confirming that.

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for either Alex or Blake?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, that's helpful to know.

**Are both Alex and Blake either:**
- **British citizens, or**
- **Entitled to live in the UK (for example, through settled status, indefinite leave to remain, or another immigration status)?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you.

Now I just need to confirm a couple of things about yourself:

**Are you aged 16 or over?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One more question:

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Before I make my assessment, let me just check the official eligibility rules to ensure I provide you with accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for your patience. Based on the information you've provided and the official eligibility rules, I can now make my assessment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great news! Based on the information you've provided, **you appear to be eligible for Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake**.

Here's a summary of my assessment:

### Alex (age 8) ✅ Eligible
- Alex is under 16 years old
- Alex is a British citizen
- Alex lives with you and you provide their day-to-day care
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

### Blake (age 9) ✅ Eligible
- Blake is under 16 years old
- Blake is a British citizen
- Blake lives with you and you provide their day-to-day care
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake

### Your circumstances
- You are over 16 years old
- You live in the UK
- You are responsible for both children

You can apply for Child Benefit online through the GOV.UK website or by post. If you have any further questions or your circumstances change, please don't hesitate to ask. Good luck with your application!

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old (under 16), is a British citizen, lives with the claimant in the UK, the claimant is responsible for Alex's day-to-day care, and no one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. The claimant is over 16 and lives in the UK. All eligibility criteria are met.

Blake is 9 years old (under 16), is a British citizen, lives with the claimant in the UK, the claimant is responsible for Blake's day-to-day care, and no one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Blake. The claimant is over 16 and lives in the UK. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant meets all the basic eligibility requirements for Child Benefit: they are over 16, live in the UK (Manchester), and are responsible for two children under 16 who live with them. Both children (Alex, age 8, and Blake, age 9) are British citizens living with the claimant, who provides their day-to-day care. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for either child. The family's circumstances satisfy all the eligibility criteria set out by the UK government.

The claimant is over 16 and lives in the UK. All eligibility criteria are met.

Blake is 9 years old (under 16), is a British citizen, lives with the claimant in the UK, the claimant is responsible for Blake's day-to-day care, and no one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Blake. The claimant is over 16 and lives in the UK. All eligibility criteria are met.

Both children (Alex, age 8, and Blake, age 9) are British citizens living with the claimant, who provides their day-to-day care. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for either child. The family's circumstances satisfy all the eligibility criteria set out by the UK government.
